cases.notes: Vendored Noto Naskh Arabic changes the visual-order glyph sequence for سلام beyond pure RTL reordering of raw cmap glyph IDs, the ticket-local arabic-shaped-glyph-run dump now pins the resulting RTL-ordered clusters, and the ticket-local arabic-shaping-plan dump now records the required Arabic default feature set for that bounded row without promoting full Arabic support.

Vendored Noto Naskh Arabic proves a bounded base-plus-mark case by tying the positioning or zero-advance check to the mark cluster itself, the ticket-local arabic-shaped-glyph-run dump now pins the shaped glyph/cluster facts for that row, and the ticket-local arabic-shaping-plan dump records the required Arabic default feature set and refusal-on-missing expectations for that bounded case.

Reviewed repo fixture gpos-missing-gdef.otf keeps Arabic base-plus-mark input on the stable generic gdef-required refusal path instead of approximating mark attachment without glyph classification data.
Single-run shaping emits the paragraph-owned bidi diagnostic instead of silently approximating mixed Arabic/LTR layout, while still returning shaped output.

remainingGates.detail: Vendored Noto Naskh Arabic now has ticket-local arabic-shaping-plan and arabic-shaped-glyph-run rows for logical text \u0644\u0627, but they still prove only bounded runtime divergence away from the raw visual-order cmap pair and do not yet close the feature-local lam-alef positive gate.
Positive cursive-attachment evidence is still proven only on the bounded reviewed M6-005 fixture family, not yet on the vendored Noto Arabic ticket-local fixture wave.

Dedicated arabic-missing-cursive/arabic-missing-mark fixtures and narrower text.shaping.arabic-* refusal codes remain absent; the reviewed generic gdef-required row is bounded evidence only, and ticket-local gsub-trace / gpos-trace dump families remain separate Arabic gates.
